data.title: Amneal gets U.S. FDA nod for Brekiya as a migraine treatment
data.description:
• Amneal Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AMRX) said on Thursday that the U.S. FDA has approved Brekiya autoinjector as an acute treatment for migraine or cluster headaches in adults.
  • The company said the product will be available for appropriate patients in the second half of 2025.
  • Brekiya is a dihydroergotamine autoinjector and has been advised by the FDA to not take it with strong CYP3A4 inhibitors including protease inhibitors and macrolide antibiotics.

data.title: Xponential Fitness falls on CEO retirement
data.description:
• Xponential Fitness (NYSE:XPOF) said on Thursday that Mark King, Chief Executive Officer, has informed the board that he intends to retire as the company’s CEO and as a director due to health reasons. 
  • To ensure a smooth transition, King will continue to serve as CEO throughout the search process for a successor until his retirement.
  • Xponential Fitness (NYSE:XPOF) shares were down more than 18% in Thursday trade.

data.title: Super Micro unveils new data center building solutions
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) on Thursday unveiled its Data Center Building Block Solutions (DCBBS), a solution for building liquid-cooled AI data centers, while reducing costs.

The company said the DCBBS would be deployed within three months and includes high-quality manufacturing capacity, management software, on-site services, and global support.

“With our total solution coverage, including designing data center layouts and network topologies, power and battery backup-units, DCBBS simplifies and accelerates AI data center buildouts leading to reduced costs and improved quality,” said CEO Charles Liang.

The maker of servers said that DCBBS would help customers save up to 40% power, reducing 60% data center footprint, and decreasing 40% water consumption, leading to 20% lower TCO.

“Supermicro offers a complete portfolio of service-level building blocks such as datacenter design, solution validation, and professional onsite deployment,” the company said, adding that it will have continued on-site support, along with a 4-hour Onsite Response time option for mission-critical uptime.

Earlier this week, the company also entered a multi-year partnership deal, valued at $20 billion, with a Saudi Arabia-based data center company.

Shares of the San Jose, Calif.-based company gained over 47% so far this year.

data.title: Workhorse signals sales momentum uptick with expanded W56 orders and cost reductions while advancing North American market coverage
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Workhorse Group Inc. (WKHS) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Rick Dauch stated that "customer demand in the commercial EV market continues to shift. But our direction here at Workhorse has never been more focused to design and build dependable, capable electric trucks, prove they perform in the field and win business by earning our customers' confidence and their purchase orders." He emphasized that execution, not hype, will drive growth, highlighting rigorous design, engineering, and partnerships as core to Workhorse's strategy.
  • Dauch announced that "year-to-date, we booked purchase orders for a total of 27 W56 step vans covering both the 178-inch and 208-inch wheelbase models." He also introduced a new partnership: "This does not include the first W56 order from Gateway Fleets for deployment across their vehicle network." He described this as laying the foundation for a long-term partnership as Gateway expands.
  • The company delivered FedEx's 2024 order of fully equipped 178-inch wheelbase W56 step vans, which are "expected to be deployed by FedEx at three terminals by the end of Q2, bringing the total number of FedEx terminals with W56 step vans up to 12 and growing." Dauch indicated ongoing engagement with FedEx and anticipation for the 2025 RFQ in Q2.
  • Additional recent deliveries include 13 W56 units to a national fleet via Revolv and W4 CC electric trucks to a Washington municipality through Sourcewell contracts, reflecting expansion in both commercial and government channels.
  • Dauch highlighted expansion of the dealer and service network, including new Ziegler Company dealers and certified service partners in major markets, and noted CMVSS certification for import and sale of vehicles into Canada.
  • On financial discipline, Dauch reported, "In Q1, we successfully reduced operating expenses by $9.3 million on a year-over-year basis. Cash preservation and capital discipline continue to be priorities."
  • Addressing capital markets, Dauch noted the completion of a reverse stock split to regain NASDAQ compliance and continued work with ATW on near-term funding needs.
  • CFO Bob Ginnan stated, "Sales, net of returns and allowances for the first quarter of 2025 were $600,000 compared to $1.3 million in the first quarter of last year. The $700,000 decrease in sales was primarily due to the Aero divestiture and decreased W4 CC and W56 truck sales."
  • Ginnan added, "We reduced operating costs by $9.3 million compared to Q1 2024," and confirmed a reduction in monthly operating cash to below $3 million.

Outlook

  • Management stated, "We will not be providing specific annual revenue guidance at this time." However, the company shared, "As of today, we have shipped 18 trucks so far in the second quarter, as Rick mentioned, and we aim to have more trucks shipped in the second quarter than we did all of last year."
  • Dauch emphasized focus on "building and shipping key customer orders, continuing to earn fleet through trust through demos and securing the next round of purchase orders, including FedEx's 2025 PO number two."
  • The team is "ramping production in step with demand, finalizing testing of our 140 kilowatt W56 variant and assessing new North American battery suppliers."

Financial Results

  • CFO Ginnan reported, "Cost of sales for the first quarter of 2025 was $5.2 million a decrease of $2.3 million compared to $7.4 million in the prior year."
  • Selling, general and administrative expense in the first quarter of 2025 was $6.8 million, down from $14.1 million in the prior year.
  • Research and development expenses during Q1 2025 were $1.5 million, a decrease from $3.5 million in the prior year.
  • Interest expense net for Q1 2025 was $5.3 million compared to $1.3 million in the prior year, reflecting higher convertible note balances.
  • As of March 31st, 2025, the company had $2.6 million of cash and cash equivalents and $27.9 million in restricted cash.
  • Ginnan noted, "During Q1, we spent about $118,000 on capital expenditures, and we expect that same level of spending to carry into the second quarter."

Q&A

  • NOT_APPLICABLE: The transcript did not contain analyst questions or management answers in the Q&A section.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were not present in the Q&A, resulting in no visible sentiment or concerns.
  • Management maintained a confident and focused tone throughout, with Dauch stating, "We have the right people, the right products and the right partners in place. We're proud of the momentum we're building and confident in our ability to scale Workhorse for the long-term."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained assertive, with an increased emphasis on momentum and recent order wins, but continued to express caution regarding external market challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company continued its policy of not providing specific annual revenue guidance, consistent with the previous quarter.
  • Management shifted from emphasizing regulatory and macro uncertainty in Q4 2024 to focusing on execution, recent order momentum, and expanding dealer/service networks in Q1 2025.
  • Key metrics such as sales and operating expenses were lower compared to the prior year, with continued cost discipline highlighted.
  • Previous quarter discussions centered more on headcount reduction and internal cost-saving measures, while the current quarter focused on new customer wins, expanded partnerships, and service network growth.
  • Analyst engagement in Q&A remained minimal in both quarters.
  • Management confidence in the product and execution increased, with multiple product validation stories and expansion into Canada.

Risks and Concerns

  • Dauch acknowledged "ongoing market challenges and dynamics, many of them outside of our control," including "fleet adoption delays, shifting government incentives and mandates and gas and charging infrastructure across the country."
  • Management reiterated a focus on "maintaining financial discipline" and "diligently to keep Workhorse on a financially viable path forward until a few commercial and state funded fleets place larger orders."
  • The company cited potential dilution from capital actions intended to secure short-term funding as necessary for survival and growth.

Final Takeaway

Workhorse management highlighted growing momentum in vehicle orders, new fleet and dealer partnerships, and continued operational discipline as the company pursues scale in the commercial EV market. With expanded product offerings, an enlarged service network, and stricter financial controls, the team remains focused on converting recent traction into lasting growth opportunities while navigating persistent market and funding headwinds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: LOBO EV shares skyrocket on agreement to supply 5,000 EVs to U.S. Market
data.description:
• LOBO EV Technologies (NASDAQ:LOBO) on Thursday announced a strategic partnership with a U.S.-based distributor in Atlanta. 
  • Under the agreement signed recently, the local distributor, the exclusive partner in US, plans to purchase and distribute 5,000 customized LOBO small size four-wheeler electric vehicles, representing estimated revenue of approximately $6 million USD. 
  • This marks an important next step in LOBO’s phased rollout across the United States, with a focus on supplying sightseeing shuttles and compact golf carts to key U.S. markets. 
  • LOBO is also planning to launch an electric and gasoline dual-power vehicle to meet US market demands for long-range transportation.
  • The company anticipates full-year 2025 revenue between $28 million and $30 million USD, with projected net income of approximately $3 million USD—reflecting a year-over-year revenue growth of up to 41.5%.
  • LOBO EV Technologies (NASDAQ:LOBO) shares were up more than 70% in Thursday trading hours. 
  •

data.title: Cisco Systems declares $0.41 dividend
data.description:
• Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) declares $0.41/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.57%
  • Payable July 23; for shareholders of record July 3; ex-div July 3.
  • See CSCO Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Short bets against S&P 500 healthcare stocks climb in April; MRNA retains top shorted ranking
MKS Instruments announces change of corporate name to MKS
data.description:
• MKS Instruments (NASDAQ:MKSI) has announced that its shareholders have approved the change of the Co.’s name to “MKS Inc.” and the change is effective May 16, 2025.
  • The Co.’s stock will continue to trade on Nasdaq under its current symbol “MKSI” and the name change will have no effect on MKS’ corporate structure or the names of its subsidiaries.

data.title: Vaycaychella expects $400K in revenue in Q1
data.description:
• Vaycaychella (OTCPK:VAYK) said it expects to report over $400K in revenue for the first quarter of 2025, as the firm benefited from significant growth in demand for its services.
  • The firm added the projected first-quarter revenue will be more than half of the entire year's revenue from 2024, when the company reported less than $700K in revenue for the whole year.
  • The company said it is optimistic that the annual revenue may exceed one million dollars.
  • The firm also confirmed that it has started work on the Rufus Rose House project, which, upon completion, may be worth $5–8 million.

data.title: Aura Biosciences announces public offering; shares down
data.description:
• Aura Biosciences (NASDAQ:AURA) launched an underwritten public offering of common stock, accompanying warrants, and pre-funded warrants for select investors.
  • All the shares of common stock, pre-funded warrants and accompanying common stock warrants to be sold in the offering will be offered by Aura.
  • AURA shares fall 6.5% premarket on Thursday. 
  • Source: Press release
